— Headline: Confounding, Causation, and the Shared Risk Factor Problem
— Headline: Practice Impact and What Remains Unsettled
— Close: What to Watch, What Is Still Unsettled

follow_up_links.title: Spontaneous Preterm Birth and Subsequent Cardiovascular Mortality: Linked Registry Cohort Study

follow_up_links.reason: RCT substudy showing detectable fetal brain MRI differences with lifestyle intervention, though small sample limits conclusions
follow_up_links.title: Effects of Mediterranean diet and stress-reduction interventions during pregnancy on fetal brain development detected using magnetic resonance imaging

follow_up_links.title: Twin-Twin Transfusion Syndrome and Related Monochorionic Disorders: Historical Perspectives, Current Controversies, and Evidence-Based Opportunities

follow_up_links.title: The effect of metformin on perinatal outcomes in pregnant women with type 2 diabetes differs by baseline insulin requirements

follow_up_links.reason: 24-year cohort showing Delphi sFGR criteria better predict adverse outcomes than ISUOG definition in MCDA twins
follow_up_links.title: Perinatal outcome of monochorionic twin pregnancy complicated by selective fetal growth restriction: ISUOG vs Delphi diagnostic criteria

key_claims.claim: Women with spontaneous preterm birth, particularly before 32 weeks, have approximately 2-3 times higher cardiovascular mortality compared to those delivering at term
Mediterranean diet or stress-reduction interventions during high-risk pregnancy produce detectable differences in fetal brain structure on late-gestation MRI
TTTS and related monochorionic disorders remain characterized by diagnostic variability and staging controversies despite evolving laser therapy evidence
Metformin's benefit in type 2 diabetes pregnancy may be modified by baseline insulin requirements, with higher-need women potentially deriving greater benefit
Delphi consensus criteria for selective fetal growth restriction outperform traditional ISUOG criteria in predicting adverse perinatal outcomes in MCDA twins
